name: Pharmacological Treatments
description: Traditional pharmaceutical treatments: verapamil, lithium, prednisone, melatonin, triptans. The established medical toolkit.

all_trials.title: Repeated GON Injections in CCH
all_trials.what_tested: Repeated greater occipital nerve (GON) steroid injections versus placebo as a preventive treatment for chronic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Still recruiting 50 participants, expected completion December 2025.
all_trials.patient_relevance: GON injections are already used by many headache specialists -- this trial will finally tell us if repeated injections provide lasting benefit for chronic CH.
all_trials.dose_tested: Repeated GON injections (steroid, specific agent not detailed)

all_trials.title: Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Low Sodium Oxybate for Nocturnal Cluster Headache Attacks
all_trials.what_tested: Low sodium oxybate (Xywav), a sleep medication, taken twice nightly to prevent nocturnal cluster headache attacks in chronic CH.
all_trials.key_result: Not yet recruiting, expected to start January 2026 with 52 participants.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Many CH attacks happen at night -- a medication that both improves sleep and prevents nocturnal attacks could be very valuable for CH patients.

all_trials.title: Response to Combined Trigeminal and Occipital Nerve Stimulation for the Preventive Treatment in Chronic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: The PRIMUS system combining trigeminal and occipital nerve stimulation for preventing chronic cluster headache in patients who failed verapamil.
all_trials.key_result: Recruiting 10 participants at two centers in the Netherlands, expected completion April 2027.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A next-generation neurostimulation device for CH patients who cannot tolerate verapamil -- still very early stage.
all_trials.dose_tested: PRIMUS combined trigeminal + occipital nerve stimulation

all_trials.title: Cluster Headache Cortivazol Injection (CHCI)
all_trials.what_tested: Cortivazol (a corticosteroid) injected at the greater occipital nerve as an add-on to verapamil for preventing cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 43 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Steroid injections at the greater occipital nerve are widely used for CH -- this study tested whether they add benefit to verapamil.
all_trials.dose_tested: Cortivazol injection at greater occipital nerve + oral verapamil

all_trials.title: Atrioventricular Block and Cluster Headache (SEVA)
all_trials.what_tested: Monitoring the cardiac side effects (specifically heart block) of high-dose verapamil, the standard first-line preventive for cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Active but no longer recruiting, with 60 participants. Expected completion September 2025.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Verapamil can cause dangerous heart rhythm problems at the high doses needed for CH -- this study quantifies that risk and could lead to better monitoring guidelines.
all_trials.dose_tested: Verapamil at CH treatment doses (higher than cardiology doses)

all_trials.title: R-Verapamil for the Prophylaxis of Episodic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: R-verapamil (the right-handed version of the molecule, which may have fewer heart side effects) for preventing episodic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated after enrolling only 1 participant out of planned enrollment. The study effectively failed to recruit.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A safer version of verapamil would be very welcome for CH patients, but this trial never got enough participants to test the idea.

all_trials.title: GON-injection for a Sooner and Better Treatment of Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Greater occipital nerve (GON) steroid injection as a FIRST-LINE preventive treatment for episodic cluster headache, potentially replacing the need for verapamil.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 70 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: If GON injection works as well as verapamil for starting a new cluster period, patients could avoid weeks of pill-taking and cardiac monitoring -- results eagerly awaited.
all_trials.dose_tested: GON steroid injection as first-line prophylaxis

all_trials.title: A Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study Examining the Use of Topiramate in the Treatment of Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Topiramate (Topamax), an anti-seizure medication, rapidly titrated to try to shorten cluster periods and prevent attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated with 60 participants enrolled but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Topiramate is sometimes used off-label for CH, but this controlled trial was terminated without providing clear evidence either way.
